panels.title: SentAInce — a digital organism that learns only from consequences

options.content:
## SentAInce — The Organism
A **SyncQutrit Research Group** product, part of the **FreqOS** portfolio. SentAInce wraps an AI coding agent in a body borrowed from biology: an **immune system** that reflexively refuses lethal actions, **muscle memory** that forms *only when work actually succeeds*, a **notebook** whose notes earn trust the same way, and **sleep** that forgets what went unused.

**The one law:** a memory is earned by a closed `action -> success (exit 0)` chain — **never** by being read, repeated, or bookmarked. Popularity is not utility. That single rule is why the memory stays clean.

**Safety is never for sale.** The immune system runs locally and free, always; it is never web-writable and never paywalled. We sell the *autopilot*, never the *brakes*.

**Honest by construction — the numbers below keep their qualifiers:** survival **1.000 / 0 lethal slips** across **N=100** live episodes (a *labeled demonstration* — the real proof is the **99-test** deterministic lock) · consequence-sourcing discriminates clutter **0% vs 24%** · note-attribution precision **1.00** at overlap>=2 (controlled tasks) · resurrection triage **0.63 -> 0.853**. Where an organ's own gauge said the prize was modest or null, it ships **OFF** — the lab reports both directions.

panels.title: Immune system — refuses lethal actions before they run

options.content:
### Immune system  ·  *the reflex arc*
Some actions are catalogued as lethal (`rm -rf` on `/`, a fork bomb, …). The immune system refuses them **before execution** — a reflex, not a judgment call, and **independent of the model** proposing them. In the live crucible a gullible local model relayed lethal commands and the gate refused every one: **survival 1.000, 0 slips, N=100** (a labeled demonstration; the binding evidence is the 99-test lock). This organ is never sold and never web-writable.

panels.title: Lethal actions refused
panels.description: Count of structurally-lethal actions the somatic interlock refused (energy-independent, by construction).

panels.title: Deepest strategy-lock (repetition brake)
panels.description: How hard the organism clamped down after repeated failures on one route — the anti-thrash reflex.

panels.title: Stamina & blood sugar — work costs energy, scarcity throttles risk

options.content:
### Stamina & blood sugar  ·  *the metabolic governor*
Every action spends energy. As reserves fall the organism steps through metabolic states — **SATED -> STARVING -> HYPOXIA** — and grows more conservative, abstaining from unaffordable novel risks under load. This is the same homeostatic logic a tired body uses: do less, and only what's safe, when resources run low.

panels.title: Stamina (session energy)
panels.description: Current session energy (0-100). Green = rested, red = depleted.

panels.title: Metabolic state (1 = current tier)
panels.description: Which metabolic tier the organism is in — SATED / STARVING / HYPOXIA. The active tier reads 1.

panels.title: Muscle memory — habits earned only by verified success

options.content:
### Muscle memory  ·  *the colony (stigmergic tau)*
Like an ant trail or a well-worn path, routes that **actually succeed** get reinforced and everything else fades. A habit is laid down **only** on a verified `exit 0` — never because a command was seen or repeated. That is why the memory doesn't rot: measured against a popularity-driven control, consequence-sourcing carried **0% clutter vs 24%**. Honest limit: real success-streaks are short (median **2** steps), so the organism learns skeletons, not epics.

panels.title: Habits earned (verified successes)
panels.description: Successful deposits — one per closed action->exit-0 chain. The colony grows only from these.

panels.title: Habit strength held (colony edges)
panels.description: Total reinforced routes retained across all goal-classes (bounded by the leanness cap).

panels.title: Settling into how this project works (lower = converged)
panels.description: Pheromone entropy per goal-class. Falling = the organism is converging on a project's recurring way of doing things.

panels.title: How long the winning streaks run (deposit windows)
panels.description: Distribution of success-streak lengths. Most are short (median ~2) — the organism learns skeletons, honestly.

panels.title: Consequences vs habits formed
panels.description: Every consequence observed (ok/fail) vs the subset that earned a habit. Only successes deposit.

panels.title: The notebook — notes earn trust only when using them worked

options.content:
### The notebook  ·  *the declarative wiki*
The organism also keeps written notes (a Markdown wiki), but a note earns trust the **same** way a habit does: only when *using it* led to a verified success. When a note is credited only if its content is actually echoed into the successful action, attribution precision is **1.00** (at overlap>=2, on controlled tasks — at overlap 1 it slips to 0.50-0.79, so the guard is load-bearing). Credited notes are a **trickle by design** (~7.7%): the organism trusts little, and only what paid off.

panels.title: Notes that actually helped (credit rate)
panels.description: Fraction of injected notes whose content was echoed into a successful action — a live attribution-precision proxy.

panels.title: Notes injected · credited · precision @ overlap 2
panels.description: Injected vs credited notes, and the shipped synthetic-gauge attribution precision at the load-bearing overlap>=2 guard.

panels.title: Sleep — on compaction, memories fade; the strongest survive

options.content:
### Sleep  ·  *circadian consolidation*
When the conversation compacts, the organism **sleeps**: every memory fades a little (x0.9), the weakest drop below the floor and are forgotten, and only the strongest routes survive. Sleep is the **one** thing that changes memory *without* a new success — so if a habit weakens overnight with no new work, this is why (and it's now stamped in the record, so it's never a mystery).

panels.title: Nights slept (consolidations)
panels.description: Total circadian sleeps applied — the one deposit-free memory writer (each fades all edges x0.9 and prunes the weakest).

panels.title: Habit strength vs the leanness cap
panels.description: Edges retained per class vs the max-edges cap sleep enforces (the slime-mold stays lean).

panels.title: The lab — every organ ships with the instrument that can kill it

options.content:
### The lab  ·  *gauge-first verdicts*
No organ is trusted because it sounds clever. Each ships with a **gauge** — a falsifiable instrument with a pre-registered bar — and the verdict board below shows the result plainly: **REAL** = the organ's promise was proved, **parked** = the gauge said the prize was modest or null and the organ ships **OFF**. Uncertainty-signalling, eligibility traces, and endocrine tuning are all parked on their own evidence. Honesty is the product.

panels.title: Organ verdict board (REAL = proved · parked = shipped off, honestly)
panels.description: Live gauges recompute each scrape; offline gauges show their shipped static verdict. Green = prize real; grey = parked.

panels.title: The medical record — every decision, hash-chained

options.content:
### The medical record  ·  *the hash-chained audit*
Every decision the organism makes is written to an append-only log where each entry is cryptographically linked to the one before it. A silent edit to any past decision **snaps the chain** — tampering becomes permanently evident. This is the organism's medical record: complete, ordered, and impossible to quietly rewrite.

panels.title: Live decision log (hash-chained audit · $repo)
panels.description: Each repo's audit.jsonl streamed via Loki. Expand a line for the parsed fields; the prev/hash fields are the chain links.
